title: elaeagnus umbellata subsp. parvifolia
intro: Elaeagnus umbellata subsp. parvifolia, also known as silverberry or wolfberry, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its silver-green leaves and small, white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high levels of vitamin C and other bioactive compounds. Consuming silverberry is said to provide various health benefits, such as boosting the immune system, reducing oxidative stress, and potentially lowering cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, Elaeagnus umbellata subsp. parvifolia can be used as a tea, tincture, or supplement, and it is also used in traditional cuisine in East Asia, often dried or used in soups and stews.
